title: B9. Polymer Adiabatic Force Law
label: mx1_review_20
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B9-polymer-adiabatic-force-law
Title: B9. Polymer Adiabatic Force Law

Problem:
Freely jointed polymer chain with n mass points joined by struts of length l.
What is the force law F(x) when thermally isolated?

Grading target:
Exact formula match on coefficient and powers.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

root_orchestration.decision_notes: Runtime converted an invalid authored response into a problem report.

grading.extracted_answer: F(x) = (n k_B T_0 / l) * (x / (n l))^(2/3) * (n l / x) is incorrect; the correct scaling for an adiabatic process in a freely jointed chain is F(x) = (n k_B T_0 / l) * (x / (n l))^(1/3) is also a common mistake. Let us re-derive: For a freely...
grading.grading_notes: The submitted response is an invalid node-authored response that fails to provide a final correct formula. It contains meta-commentary about incorrect formulas and does not match the authoritative reference F(x) = 3E(0)x/(nl)^2.
